rules.instruction: A green test suite does not prove that the requested task was implemented.
Compare task requirements with the actual Git diff and changed files.
Reject coding tasks that produce no allowed-file changes.
Reject changes outside the allowed writable file set.
Reject tests that do not invoke production behavior or use placeholders.
Reject unexpected removal of existing module-level functions or classes.
Evaluate Builder status first. Run downstream checks only after Builder success.
Test execution must not be contaminated by temporary shell variables such as OPENAI_API_BASE.
A test run that fails during collection is a failed review even when no test body has executed.
Treat new test warnings as review findings. Resolve avoidable warnings before accepting the task.
For every newly imported or referenced symbol, verify that its definition exists in the resulting code.
When a callable signature changes, inspect all repository call sites for missing or obsolete arguments.
Check that status, passed flags, return codes, and change flags form logically consistent combinations.
When reviewing tests, verify that expected exception types match the actual behavior of the production implementation.
